name: byos-agent-optional-pattern
description: scaffold_skill.py always emits prism: block; agent is optional hint. validate_skill.py treats missing agent as non-error; validates agent IF present (warning only). prism: block = opt-in to discovery, agent = informational hint only.

name: byos-imperative-skill-instruction
description: _format_discovered_skills uses MANDATORY imperative language: 'MANDATORY: You MUST invoke relevant skills using the Skill tool before completing your task.' Test updated to assert MANDATORY present (not absent).

name: byos-skill-relevance-threshold
description: _format_discovered_skills() presents skills as available-if-relevant with 1% threshold — agent decides based on relevance, not forced to invoke all. Updated wording: 'The following skills are available. Invoke any skill if there is even a 1% chance it is relevant.'

name: byos-skill-invocation-threshold
description: _format_discovered_skills wording: present skills as available-if-relevant with 1% threshold, not unconditional MANDATORY force-all. Confirmed: MANDATORY force-all causes wasted Skill calls every step; weak 'review and invoke' causes agents to skip all skills. The 1% threshold balances both failure modes.

content: Skill command files follow validate-cli.md pattern: frontmatter description, Usage section, Execute bash block with CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT, per-command docs with examples, Storage and Dependencies sections.

prism-bug skill pattern: skill captures state file, session transcript JSONL, gate YAML, git context, plugin version — uploads full transcript as Gist via gh gist create, creates GitHub Issue via gh issue create --repo siegeon/.prism with labels bug,prism-session — each source handled independently so missing files are noted but not fatal

description: prism-bug.py transcript parser used top-level type field but Claude Code JSONL uses role+content[] blocks.
resolution: Iterate content array, extract blocks by type (text/tool_use/tool_result) from each role entry.

name: create-issue-label-fallback
description: prism-bug create_issue() label fallback: try with labels; if rc\!=0 retry without 'prism-session'; if still fails retry with no labels — handles repos where label doesn't exist.

content: using-brain SKILL.md teaches Claude automatic Brain query patterns: explain before editing unfamiliar files, search before answering codebase questions, graph for dependency tracing, search for error debugging.

name: skills-directive-in-core-steps
description: All 6 core-steps/*.md now have a ## Skills section directing agents to use the Skill tool before implementing manually. _format_discovered_skills() emits a '## Available Skills' header. Tests: test_core_steps_have_skills_directive (spec conformance) and test_skills_section_header_in_assembled_instruction.

name: skill-discovery-diagnostic-logging
description: discover_prism_skills logs to stderr with [skill-discovery] prefix: paths scanned, skills found/skipped, and total count. Enables debugging of skill pipeline without affecting stdout.

name: skill-discovery-stderr-diagnostics
description: discover_prism_skills() logs paths scanned, skills found/skipped to stderr. _format_discovered_skills uses MANDATORY imperative wording.

name: prism-bug-hooks-path
description: prism-bug diagnostic expansion pattern: add _add_hooks_to_path() to insert plugin hooks dir into sys.path for importing brain_engine/conductor_engine/prism_loop_context/prism_stop_hook; each collector catches Exception and returns markdown string describing failure so report is never broken by missing deps

name: brain-api-correct-attributes
description: prism-bug.py Brain API: SQLite connection is brain._brain (not brain._conn); system_context signature is system_context(story_file=None, persona=None, limit=8) — no 'role' or 'step' params; step_history entries use 'i' for step index, not 'step'

name: prism-bug-diagnostic-collectors
description: prism-bug.py v3.5.0 has 13 diagnostic collectors: state, plugin_version, transcript_excerpt, gate_results, git_context, brain_status, conductor_status, skill_discovery, test_runner_detection, step_history, plugin_cache_path, session_start_output, hook_execution. Windows path fix: replaces both / and \ in CWD mangling.

name: byos-test-skill-detection
description: detect_test_runner() in prism_stop_hook.py checks .claude/skills/ for skill dirs whose name matches test patterns (r'(^|[-_])test(s?)([-_]|$)') before hardcoded runners. Extracts bash command from ## Execute section of SKILL.md. Returns type='byos', lint=None.

content: LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS set in prism_loop_context.py guards skill_text injection in build_agent_instruction(): review_previous_notes and verify_plan skip BYOS skills to preserve token budget (these steps read handoff/stop in ~10K tokens). Tests for skill injection must skip LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS steps. Core-step tests (skills_directive, brain_examples) also skip LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS.

discover_prism_skills() must NOT write to stderr: Claude Code hooks capture stderr and inject it into the conversation, causing the skills block to appear twice. All [skill-discovery] print statements removed.

LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS = {review_previous_notes, verify_plan}: build_agent_instruction() skips BYOS skill injection for these steps. They are context-only steps that don't perform feature work, so skill invocation wastes tokens. Guard: 'if skill_text and step_id not in LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS'.

Conductor-driven skill filtering: Conductor.select_relevant_skills(step_id, agent, all_skills) returns top 3-5 skills via Brain.get_skill_scores() frequency ranking (warm-start) or _STEP_SKILL_KEYWORDS keyword heuristic (cold-start). prism_loop_context.build_agent_instruction(filtered_skills=) accepts pre-filtered list; uses 'Consider these relevant skills' wording vs MANDATORY for full discovery.
Brain.get_skill_scores() queries skill_usage table for COUNT(*) per skill_name across all sessions. Returns dict[str,int]. Used by Conductor.select_relevant_skills() for warm-start ranking.

Skill progressive disclosure pattern: thin SKILL.md (<=20 lines) contains frontmatter + 1-line summary + numbered steps + reference link to instructions.md. Full content moves to instructions.md verbatim. Tier flags: user-invocable:false for Tier 2, disable-model-invocation:true for SM-agent-only skills.

QA skill progressive-disclosure pattern: SKILL.md ≤16 lines (frontmatter+summary+5 Quick Start steps+reference link); full content in instructions.md; disable-model-invocation: true on all QA-scoped skills; descriptions <120 chars for Conductor matching

skill-refactor thin SKILL.md pattern: ≤20 lines = frontmatter (with disable-model-invocation: true) + 1-line summary + ## Steps block + 'See [instructions.md](./instructions.md) for ...' reference link. Full body moves to instructions.md in same directory.

skill consolidation pattern: merge N skills into 1 by writing instructions.md with full content, thin SKILL.md ≤20 lines with disable-model-invocation: true, progressive disclosure via reference link. Delete originals with git rm -r.

validate-all.py find_script() path: after validate consolidation, candidates list must use 'skills/validate/scripts' not 'skills/validate-markdown-refs/scripts' — update when moving scripts to consolidated dir.

Skill progressive disclosure: SKILL.md ≤20 lines (frontmatter + summary + steps + reference link), detailed instructions in instructions.md. Only 5 skills are model-invocable (prism-loop, prism-bug, remember, correct-course, create-next-story). All others have disable-model-invocation: true. Conductor handles step-specific skill injection.

disable-model-invocation: true blocks Skill tool invocation entirely — removed from 22 agent-scoped skills (Tiers 1-3), kept only on 10 dev tools (Tier 4: validate, version-bump, byos, skill-builder, hooks-manager, agent-builder, init-context, conductor, brain, jira).

Phase-aware skill scoring: _infer_skill_phase maps priority 20-29=BUILD,30-39=VERIFY,40-49=SHIP,50-59=OPERATE,else=TOP_LEVEL. _score_skill adds phase(+10)+keyword(+3)+brain_usage+tiebreak. Replaces boolean substring match in select_relevant_skills.

Phase-aware skill matching: _infer_skill_phase maps priority ranges to lifecycle phases (10-19=top-level, 20-29=BUILD, 30-39=VERIFY, 40-49=SHIP, 50-59=OPERATE). _score_skill provides additive scoring (phase+keyword+brain+tiebreak). select_relevant_skills unified to single scored ranking path.
Skill tool input uses 'skill' key (not 'name'): block.get('input',{}).get('skill','') extracts the skill name from a Skill tool_use transcript block

Conductor.record_outcome skill recording: pass skill_names list and session_id in metrics dict; conductor iterates and calls brain.record_skill_usage(session_id, name, step_id) for each non-empty name

skills-placement: Available Skills block in build_agent_instruction() is injected right after role card (before step body), not at the bottom. This ensures agents see skills before starting manual work. LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS are still excluded. Core step files reference 'Available Skills listed above' with step-specific directives (e.g. /test for GREEN/RED, /checkin for commit) instead of generic 'Check for available skills' language.

prism-bug Skill Usage Analysis: collect_skill_usage_analysis(transcript_path) extracts Skill tool_use blocks (inp['skill']), Bash tool_use blocks (inp['command']), computes adoption% (invoked/available), and lists un-invoked skills (adoption gap)

skill-bypass-enforcement: _parse_skill_frontmatter extracts optional replaces: field; _format_discovered_skills appends DO NOT run language per skill when replaces is set; detect_skill_bypass() in stop hook scans Bash tool_use blocks for commands matching replaces fields and prepends warnings to next-step block reason
prism-done skill: SKILL.md frontmatter includes author: PRISM field; scripts/prism-done.py main() reconfigures stdout/stderr to UTF-8 with errors=replace before parsing args
